theory.intro: The area of a rectangle is the amount of surface it covers, found by multiplying its length by its width.
facts: Area is measured in square units: cm$^2$, m$^2$, km$^2$.
A square is a rectangle with equal sides, so its area is **side $\times$ side**.
steps: Read the length and the width (use the same unit for both).
Write the answer with squared units.

development.q: Find the area of this rectangle.
A rectangular garden is $15$ m long and $8$ m wide. Find its area.
A square tile has side $25$ cm. Find its area.
Find the area of this rectangle.
A wall is $6$ m long and $250$ cm high. Find its area in m$^2$.
A rectangle is $20$ cm by $9$ cm. Find its area.
Find the area of this rectangle.
A room measures $4$ m by $3$ m. Carpet costs \$$40$ per m$^2$. Find the total cost.
A rectangle has area $48\text{ cm}^2$ and length $8$ cm. Find its width.

mastery.q: A rectangular floor is $7.5$ m by $4$ m. Tiles are $0.5$ m by $0.5$ m. How many tiles cover the floor?
A rectangle has area $72\text{ cm}^2$ and width $6$ cm. Find its length and perimeter.
A path $2$ m wide surrounds a $10$ m by $6$ m pool. Find the area of the path only.
A square garden has side $9$ m. A second square garden has double the side. How many times bigger is its area?
A wall $5$ m by $3$ m has a $2$ m by $1$ m window. Find the area to be painted.
Two rectangles have the same area of $36\text{ cm}^2$. One is $4$ cm by $9$ cm. Give whole-number dimensions for another.
A rectangular lawn is $24$ m by $15$ m. Find its area in m$^2$, then in hectares.
A rectangle is $3$ times as long as it is wide and has area $108\text{ cm}^2$. Find its dimensions.
A poster is $80$ cm by $60$ cm. It is reduced so each side is halved. Find the new area.
A floor $6$ m by $5$ m is to be carpeted at \$$35$ per m$^2$ plus a \$$50$ fitting fee. Find the total cost.
